tools.name: golangci-lint
tools.notes: Uses bodyclose, copyloopvar, depguard, errorlint, gocritic, gosec, importas, misspell, revive, staticcheck, testifylint, unconvert linters. Also enforces gci import ordering and gofumpt formatting.

tools.name: cargo fmt
tools.scope: candle-binding, ml-binding, nlp-binding, onnx-binding Rust crates

linting.notes: Pre-commit hooks enforce all linters. Run 'make precommit-check' to run all pre-commit hooks.

testing.framework: Go standard testing with Ginkgo/Gomega BDD framework
env_vars: SKIP_MILVUS_TESTS=true - skips Milvus-dependent tests (default: true locally, false in CI)
SKIP_REDIS_TESTS=true - skips Redis-dependent tests (default: true locally, false in CI)
SR_TEST_MODE=true - enables test mode for router
HF_TOKEN - HuggingFace token for downloading gated models
LD_LIBRARY_PATH - must include paths to Rust .so libraries
testing.notes: Tests are split: test-binding-minimal runs minimal classifier tests, test-binding-lora runs LoRA tests, test-semantic-router runs Go unit tests. Full test suite requires Milvus and Redis services running. Uses Ginkgo BDD framework with Gomega matchers.

gating_checks.name: test-and-build
gating_checks.command: make test (which runs: make vet check-go-mod-tidy download-models test-binding-minimal test-semantic-router clean-minimal-models download-models-lora test-binding-lora)
gating_checks.notes: Requires Rust 1.90, Go 1.24, make, build-essential, pkg-config. Starts Milvus and Redis services. Uses /mnt for model storage to avoid disk space issues.

gating_checks.name: pre-commit
gating_checks.command: make precommit-check (runs all pre-commit hooks including go-lint, cargo fmt, black, eslint, yamllint, markdown-lint, shellcheck, codespell)
gating_checks.notes: Also runs 'make agent-fast-gate' to check changed files with agent tooling.

gating_checks.name: check-go-mod-tidy
gating_checks.notes: Verifies all Go modules have tidy go.mod and go.sum files.

gating_checks.name: dashboard-test
gating_checks.notes: Only runs when dashboard code changes. Requires Node.js 23, Go 1.24, golangci-lint.

advisory_checks.name: integration-test-k8s
advisory_checks.notes: Runs on changes to helm, k8s, or docker files. Requires Kind cluster.

ci.notes: CI uses path-based change detection via ci-changes.yml workflow. Nightly runs publish Docker images. PR builds cache Go deps, Rust deps, Node deps, and models (up to ~75GB on /mnt).

build.system: Make with modular makefiles in tools/make/
build.install_command: make rust-ci (for CI/CPU-only) or make rust (for local/CUDA)
build.build_command: make build (builds Rust libs + Go router binary)
build.notes: Build requires: Rust 1.90, Go 1.24, make, build-essential, pkg-config. Rust libraries (candle-binding, ml-binding, nlp-binding) must be built before Go code. Use 'make build-router' for just the router binary. Use 'make rust-ci' for CPU-only builds in CI.

conventions.test_naming: Go: TestXxx functions or Ginkgo Describe/It blocks. Rust: #[test] fn test_xxx
conventions.import_style: Go imports organized by gci: standard, default, prefix(github.com/vllm-project/semantic-router). Enforced by golangci-lint.
conventions.notes: Go tests use Ginkgo/Gomega BDD style. Test files often have _test suffix with parallel test support. Rust tests in same file as implementation or in tests/ subdirectory.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y make build-essential pkg-config git curl shellcheck python3 python3-pip

container_recipe.notes: Container must have bind mount to repo at /app with :Z flag for SELinux. Rust build takes ~5-10 minutes first time. Go lint has existing issues that need fixing. Full test suite requires external services (Milvus, Redis) and model downloads.

gaps: Full test suite requires external services (Milvus vector DB on port 19530, Redis on port 6379) - not validated in container
Model downloads not tested - would require 10+ GB downloads and HuggingFace token for gated models
Go linting has 11 existing issues (8 errorlint, 2 gci, 1 gocritic) that would block pre-commit hooks
Dashboard frontend testing not validated (requires Node.js setup)
E2E integration tests require full stack (router + envoy + vLLM) - not validated
Some tests require GPU/CUDA environment - container used CPU-only mode
Pre-commit hooks not fully tested (would require installing all language toolchains)
